description: A second, content-distinct malformed payload (different intake_id, lead_email, reply_text, and an explicit empty-string event_type rather than a missing one) used alongside fixture 11 to confirm reports/UNRESOLVED_ITEMS.md U3 is resolved: Workflow 1 Section E1's identifier_poor_hash scheme hashes raw_payload itself, so two different malformed payloads get DIFFERENT idempotency_key values and are each processed independently as op-malformed/REJECTED, instead of the second being misread as a duplicate of the first.

expected.notes: Compared against fixture 11 (also MALFORMED, different intake_id/lead_email/reply_text and an explicit empty-string event_type instead of a missing one): the run_synthetic_tests.js main loop performs a cross-check confirming idempotency.idempotency_key for fixture 11 and fixture 13 are NOT equal (different canonicalPayloadHash(raw_payload) -> different identifier_poor_hash key), and both fixtures independently reach op-malformed/REJECTED rather than either being misread as a duplicate of the other. This resolves reports/UNRESOLVED_ITEMS.md U3.
